completed_tasks.name: Add failing small-D area=D regression test (RED)
Fix Solve infeasible small-D case so integral equals D (GREEN)
Per-frame SOI-arrival clamp + warp timeout backstop in _WarpProcess (WR-02)

decisions.decision: Solve small-D fix scales both vLaunch and vTerminal by k=D/rampContribution (vCruise=0) when rampContribution>D
decisions.rationale: Keeps the smoothstep ramp shape and the Solve signature intact while making the velocity integral = D by construction (area=D invariant). Chosen over shortening ramp duration or a special near-arrival profile.

decisions.decision: Anti-tunnel implemented as disengage-before-motion guard in _WarpProcess (step >= remainingToSoi -> DisengageWarp; return)
decisions.rationale: Clamps the per-frame step to the SOI arrival point (ROADMAP WR-02) robust to any frame size; keeps warp/manual motion paths uniform rather than special-casing ApplyMotion.
Bounded absolute timeout guarantees a degenerate/stranded warp terminates shortly after planned T_sel.

next_action: Run /gsd-verify-work 08 to re-verify the two previously-failed truths (P3-TIMING small-D area=D, and WR-02 frame-boundary anti-tunnel) now that gap-closure plan 08-03 is complete. All 67 unit tests pass and EcoSpace.csproj builds.
context_notes: Phase 08 gap-closure (plan 08-03) fully executed and committed in this session. Both Phase-8 verification BLOCKERs are closed: (1) WarpMotionProfile.Solve area=D invariant for small-D legs, (2) FlightController._WarpProcess per-frame SOI clamp + timeout. Execution ran INLINE (sequential) because the Agent/Task subagent tool was unavailable in this runtime context — that is the documented execute-phase fallback, not an error. The phase is at a clean stopping point: 3/3 plans have SUMMARYs, ROADMAP 08-03 is checked, STATE progress recalculated to 100%. NOTE: state.advance-plan returned a parse error ('Cannot parse Current Plan or Total Plans in Phase from STATE.md') — STATE progress was still recalculated via state.update-progress, but STATE.md may have a minor Current-Plan-counter formatting quirk worth a glance on resume. The only outstanding (optional, WARNING-level, out-of-scope) item is the WarpConfirmationScreen.cs v_c display: it uses ManualMaxSpeed for both ramp endpoints while EngageWarp uses _easedSpeed as vLaunch, so the displayed cruise speed is inaccurate when the ship is not already at ManualMaxSpeed when the panel opens. Not a blocker; address only if desired.
